canonical_question: What are the key Dodd-Frank provisions -- Volcker Rule, swap dealer registration, resolution planning?
aliases: Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform key requirements
SIFI designation and resolution planning obligations
constraints: US federal law only -- applies to US-chartered banks, US branches of foreign banks, and non-US entities with substantial US swap dealing activity
CFPB funding halved by One Big Beautiful Bill Act (July 2025); enforcement capacity significantly reduced
SIFI designation applies only to bank holding companies with $250B+ in consolidated assets; Fed retains discretion for $100B-$250B firms
Entities must first determine whether they meet swap dealer, major swap participant, or SIFI thresholds
Basel III capital requirements, SEC regulations, and state-level banking laws overlay Dodd-Frank obligations

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User is asking about EU financial regulation (MiFID II, EMIR, CRD)
User is asking about data privacy regulation (GDPR, CCPA)
User is asking about anti-money laundering (BSA/AML)

related_to.label: GDPR Compliance Requirements (different regulatory domain, similar compliance structure)

inputs_needed.question: What type of financial institution is the user asking about?
options: Bank holding company ($250B+ assets -- full Dodd-Frank applies)
Bank holding company ($100B-$250B -- Fed discretion on enhanced standards)
Bank holding company (under $100B -- most enhanced standards do not apply)
Community bank (under $10B -- Volcker Rule exemption applies)
Swap dealer or major swap participant (Title VII registration required)
Nonbank financial company (FSOC may designate as SIFI)

inputs_needed.question: Which Dodd-Frank provision is the user asking about?
options: Swap dealer registration and OTC derivatives (Title VII)
Resolution planning and living wills (Title I)
SIFI designation and enhanced prudential standards
General overview of all major provisions

applies_to.user_segment: Banks, bank holding companies, nonbank SIFIs, swap dealers, major swap participants, and financial institutions operating in the US
applies_to.context: Post-2008 financial regulatory framework governing systemic risk, proprietary trading, OTC derivatives, consumer protection, and orderly liquidation
